channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: NASA did eventually solve Artemis II’s Outlook glitch
item.description: On Thursday, during Artemis II's journey to the Moon, commander Reid Wiseman ran into a tech issue some of us back on Earth can relate to: Microsoft Outlook wasn't working. In a conversation...

item.title: You’ll Never Forget OOPS Relationships Again
item.description: Every strong system you build starts with strong fundamentals. Most people read these concepts. Few actually remember them in interviews. Here’s the simplest way to lock them in forever 👇 1....

item.title: How we researched 30 meal kit companies and what we found
item.description: The UK meal kit delivery market is brutal. Over half the companies we researched no longer exist. But the survivors share patterns that the casualties consistently missed. We ran our full pipeline...

item.title: Show HN: We're building an AI hedge fund
item.description: Article URL: https://rallies.ai/arena Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47631929 Points: 3 # Comments: 1

item.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
item.description: In addition to Lightcap's new role, OpenAI CMO Kate Rouch will be stepping away from the company to focus on cancer recovery, with a plan to return when her health allows.

item.title: Why OpenAI Buying TBPN Matters More Than It Looks
item.description: OpenAI’s acquisition of TBPN, the fast-rising tech talk show founded by John Coogan and Jordi Hays, looks odd at first glance. This is the company behind ChatGPT and frontier model research, not a...

item.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: reports
item.description: Anthropic has purchased the stealth biotech AI startup Coefficient Bio in a $400 million stock deal, according to The Information and Eric Newcomer.

item.title: The Docker Dependency Problem No One Talks About (But Everyone Feels)
item.description: Docker is often described as the solution to “it works on my machine.” And to be fair, it really does solve a lot of pain. But after years of using it in real-world systems, I’ve noticed...

item.title: **Title: The Hidden Relationships That Keep Modern IT Systems Alive**
item.description: In IT, we often talk about tools, frameworks, and architectures like they exist in isolation—Kubernetes here, microservices there, CI/CD pipelines somewhere in between. But in reality, nothing in...

item.title: Your AI sales agent has a problem
item.description: Your AI sales agent has a problem. It's smart enough to write personalized emails, research companies, and craft outreach sequences. But it's working with dead data. Contact databases decay 30%...

item.title: ⚖️ AI Is Transforming Legal Practice in Romania — Why Lawyers Who Ignore It Are Already Falling Behind
item.description: ⚖️ AI Is Transforming Legal Practice in Romania — And Most Lawyers Aren't Ready The legal profession has survived centuries of change. From handwritten scrolls to typewriters, from physical...

item.title: OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
item.description: OpenAI is undergoing another round of C-suite changes, according to an internal memo viewed by The Verge. Fidji Simo, OpenAI's CEO of AGI deployment - who was until recently the company's CEO of...

item.title: Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC
item.description: With the midterms right around the corner, the new group is positioned to back candidates who support the AI company's policy agenda.

item.title: Oracle Files H-1B Visa Petitions Amid Mass Layoffs
item.description: Article URL: https://nationaltoday.com/us/tx/austin/news/2026/04/03/oracle-files-thousands-of-h-1b-visa-petitions-amid-mass-layoffs/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47631732...

item.title: Automatic Textbook Formalization
item.description: Article URL: https://github.com/facebookresearch/repoprover Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47631648 Points: 5 # Comments: 1

item.title: AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
item.description: Meta, Microsoft, and Google are all betting big on new natural gas power plants to run their AI data centers. They may regret it.

item.title: OpenAI’s Fidji Simo Is Taking Medical Leave Amid an Executive Shake-Up
item.description: The company is undergoing major leadership restructuring as its CEO of AGI deployment goes on leave for “several weeks.”

item.title: People would rather have an Amazon warehouse in their backyard than a data center
item.description: A new poll shows that the debate over data centers is far from settled.

item.title: Lenovo Legion Go 2 suddenly costs $650 more as RAMageddon lays waste to gaming hardware
item.description: Remember when we thought the Legion Go 2 was expensive at $1,099 and up? Those were the days - Best Buy is now listing Lenovo's handheld for $1,499 with a Ryzen Z2 or $1,999 with a Z2 Extreme. The...

item.title: China-Linked TA416 Targets European Governments with PlugX and OAuth-Based Phishing
item.description: A China-aligned threat actor has set its sights on European government and diplomatic organizations since mid-2025, following a two-year period of minimal targeting in the region. The campaign has...

item.title: The best iPad deals you can get right now
item.description: While the best iPad deals usually land during major sale events like Black Friday, many great iPad deals are available outside of those moments. The day-to-day discounts come and go like the...

item.title: Four things we’d need to put data centers in space
item.description: MIT Technology Review Explains: Let our writers untangle the complex, messy world of technology to help you understand what’s coming next. You can read more from the series here. In January, Elon...

item.title: Mercedes adds steer-by-wire — and a dang steering yoke — to the EQS
item.description: Steer-by-wire, in which a car can be steered electronically rather than through a physical connection between the steering wheel and steering rack, is coming to Mercedes-Benz. The German automaker...

item.title: Microsoft Details Cookie-Controlled PHP Web Shells Persisting via Cron on Linux Servers
item.description: Threat actors are increasingly using HTTP cookies as a control channel for PHP-based web shells on Linux servers and to achieve remote code execution, according to findings from the Microsoft...

item.title: Anker’s small, five-port travel adapter is down to its best price yet
item.description: Few things kill the vibe of your relaxing spring break abroad faster than realizing you forgot a way to keep things charged. Anker’s Nano Travel Adapter makes it easy to charge your phone, camera,...

item.title: I saved a doomed Windows laptop by embracing Linux
item.description: Two weeks ago I set aside my M4 MacBook Air and picked up a nine-year-old ThinkPad. It's one of an estimated 200 to 400 million Windows 10 PCs that don't meet Microsoft's requirements for Windows...

item.title: The Facebook insider building content moderation for the AI era
item.description: Moonbounce has raised $12 million to grow its AI control engine that converts content moderation policies into consistent, predictable AI behavior.

item.title: Waiting for Trump Phone
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. Crickets. Despite recent signs of life, another...

item.title: How the Apple Watch defined modern health tech
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent every Friday from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your...

item.title: Apple’s best product ever
item.description: All week, we've been asking you to help us rank the 50 best products Apple ever made, as we mark the company's 50th anniversary. Thanks to everyone who pitched in - we ended up with more than 1.6...
